title: Pyrrocoma howellii
intro: Pyrrocoma howellii, also known as Howell's pyrrocoma, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. Pyrrocoma howellii contains bioactive constituents including sesquiterpene lactones, particularly pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit potent biological activities. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are commonly used to treat topical skin conditions and to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Pyrrocoma howellii is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Asterales, and the family Asteraceae. Its classification also includes the subfamily Helenioideae and the tribe Madieae. The genus Pyrrocoma is a group of flowering plants native to the western United States. The genus Pyrrocoma is closely related to other genera in the tribe Madieae, including Madia and Io. Pyrrocoma howellii is often placed in the same genus as other species of Pyrrocoma.
